name: Cauliflower Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the cauliflower in boiling, salted water until tender, then drain well.
2. Melt the butter in a medium saucepan, blend in the flour, and cook, stirring over medium heat until bubbly. Do not let the mixture brown.
3. In another saucepan, bring the milk to a boil.
4. Add the hot milk to the butter-flour mixture and cook, stirring over medium heat until the sauce thickens and is smooth. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg to taste.
5. Remove from heat.
6. Lightly beat the egg yolks and stir into the sauce. Then stir in the cheese and cauliflower until smooth.
7. In a large bowl, beat the egg whites until stiff but not dry.
8. Gently fold the cauliflower mixture into the beaten egg whites.
9. Pour the batter into a buttered 2-quart (2 L) souffle dish.
10. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 30 to 40 minutes, or until puffed, firm, and lightly browned.
11. Serve immediately.
SERVES: 4-6
